entry.title: Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Business Context

The process for advanced intercompany stock transfer allows you to transfer physical stock from one plant to another plant in the same system and the plants are owned by different companies.

A new functionality is introduced in SAP S/4HANA OP2022, OP2023, and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, for intercompany stock transport. While the classic intercompany stock transport order remains available, the advanced intercompany stock transport order offers several additional benefits:

  • Track Sales KPIs
  • Reuse Valuated Stock in Transit (VSIT) for transfer of control
  • Utilize VSIT for both the delivering and receiving companies
  • Support the matching principle for financial postings, ensuring simultaneous recognition of Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) and revenue, thereby achieving compliance with IFRS 15.

Process:

The process starts with the creation of a Stock Transport Order (STO). The supplier in the purchase order is a business partner representing a plant from a different company code. The purchase order is created using the document type NBIC (Intercompany Stock Transfer STO).

Once the purchase order is processed, a Sales Order is immediately created with the document type CBIC (Intercompany Order).

SAP provides an app called “Monitor Value Chains”, which displays the entire document flow.

Next, an outbound delivery is created against the STO using transaction VL10B, followed by Post Goods Issue (PGI) using transaction VL02N. Upon PGI, two material documents are automatically created in the background:

  1. A material document with movement type 681 (Stock Transfer to Issuing Valuated Cross-Company-Code Stock in Transit (CST)).
  2. Another material document with movement type 685 (Stock Transfer from Issuing Valuated CST).

Simultaneously, another material document is created in the receiving plant with movement type 107 (Goods Receipt in Valuated Goods Receipt Blocked Stock). This material document is generated by a background job based on the planned execution date.

Once the material reaches the receiving plant, the receiving team posts the Goods Receipt using transaction VL02N, resulting in a material document with movement type 109 (Goods Receipt from Valuated Goods Receipt Blocked Stock).

The receiving company then creates an invoice using transaction VF01 against the outbound delivery. Subsequently, an invoice is automatically created in the supplying company, and a supplier invoice is posted in the receiving plant (via an automatic MIRO process).

Document Flow:

The entire document flow is visible in the “Monitor Value Chains” app.

You can monitor related logs using transaction SLG1 with the following parameters:

  • Object: VCM
  • Subobject: *

Configuration

  1. Create BP for suppling plant and receiving plant.
  2. Extend supplier role for supplying plant and customer role for receiving plant.
  3. Use standard document type – NBIC for creating STO.

4. Use delivery type – NCC3 for outbound delivery and – NCC3 for delivery item category.

5. Use standard schedule lien category – N3

6. Configure Delivery Type & Availability Check Procedure by Plant.

7. Use Standard billing type – IV2S for the cancellation of the invoice and IV2 for intercompany invoice and IV3 for intercompany proforma invoice

8. Use sales order type – CBIC for the intercompany sales order.

9. Define schedule line category – CI for the intercompany sales order

10. Define jobs to Execute Scheduled Process Steps

IMG>ABAP Platform>Application Server>System Administration>Activation of Scope-Dependent Background Job Definitions

11. Tcode : SJOBREPO. Customize the job

12. Activate event type linkage

SPRO -> ABAP Platform -> SAP Gateway Service Enablement → Backend OData Channel → Backend Event Publisher Administration → Event Type Linkages.

13. Assign Value Chain Category to Sales Document Type

SPRO -> Sales and Distribution -> Sales → Sales Documents → Integration with Value Chain Monitoring → Assign Value Chain Category to Sales Document Type

14. Assign Value Chain Category to Item Category.

SPRO -> Sales and Distribution -> Sales → Sales Documents → Integration with Value Chain Monitoring → Assign Value Chain Category to Item Category.

15. Assign Document Type and Item Category to Value Chain.

SPRO -> Sales and Distribution -> Sales → Sales Documents → Integration with Value Chain Monitoring → Assign Document Type and Item Category to Value Chain.

 

16. Enable Value Chain Monitoring for Purchase Orders.

SPRO -> Materials Management → Purchasing → Purchase Order → Advanced Intercompany Processes → Enable Value Chain Monitoring for Purchase Orders

17. Assign tax Codes

SPRO -> Materials Management → Logistics Invoice Verification → Advanced Intercompany Processes → Assign Tax Codes for the Transfer of Supplier Invoices.

18. Configure Program Parameter Configuration IDs

SPRO -> Materials Management → Logistics Invoice Verification → Advanced Intercompany Processes → Configure Program Parameters

19. Assign Program Parameter

SPRO -> Materials Management → Logistics Invoice Verification → Advanced Intercompany Processes → Assign Program Parameter

20. Configure Posting Date for Advanced Intercompany Invoices

SPRO -> Materials Management → Logistics Invoice Verification → Advanced Intercompany Processes → Configure Posting Date for Advanced Intercompany Invoices

Thanks,

Ranadeep Basak

entry.title: Detailed Scheduling Missing for Planned Orders After MRP Run - SAP Upgrade
entry.content:
Hello all,

We recently upgraded our SAP system to the latest version, retaining all master data and customizations as they were before. However, I’m encountering an issue with detailed scheduling for planned orders during MRP runs:

  • Issue Description:
    When MRP creates a planned order, the capacity requirements are not scheduled, and the Detailed Scheduling tab is missing in the planned order. Specifically:

    • Entries in KBKO and KBED tables (capacity requirements) are not created.
    • The BEDID (Capacity Requirement ID) is not passed to the PLAF table.

    However, if I manually open the planned order created by MRP and click the Scheduling button:

    • Capacity requirements are scheduled.
    • Entries in KBKO and KBED are created.
    • The BEDID is updated in the PLAF table.

  • Additional Context:

    1. This issue only occurs in the updated system; it was working fine before the upgrade - MRP did generate scheduling and the table entries created.
    2. It still works correctly in our quality system, which is on the previous SAP version.
    3. I have tested MRP via MD01N and SAPAPO Product View, but detailed scheduling is not triggered in either case.

  • Troubleshooting Attempts:

    • Debugged PPH_MRP_START, but couldn’t find the root cause.
    • Verified that all customizing and master data are unchanged from the previous version.

  • Question:
    Could you help me understand:

    1. Which ABAP program, function module, or method generates the capacity requirements entries in KBKO and KBED during MRP run?
    2. How can I debug this process further to identify what’s not being triggered after the upgrade?

Any insights or suggestions to debug the issue backward would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you in advance for your help.

SCM APO Production Planning and Detailed Scheduling (PP/DS)

entry.title: SAP Field Service Management 2411 - Available Now!
entry.content:
SAP Field Service Management 2411 builds on our strategic investment in generative AI for field service organizations, now with extended multilingual AI support. In addition, we introduced several new features designed to simplify user workflows, strengthen integration, and increase functionality. In this blog, you can find an overview of the most notable features, along with a demo video showcasing key highlights. For a complete list of all the features in the 2411 release, click here. In this blog, I will highlight the key features that will help enhance how you manage field services, including:

  • Generative AI: Multilingual support (French and German) for the Activity Summary, Equipment Insights, and Natural Language Filtering.
  • User Experience enhancements: New Morning Horizon theme, Business Partner enhancements, manage work time in the Time & Material Journal, extended sort and filter capabilities, extended customization capabilities, and more.
  • Service Map: Visualize activity details on hover, and a simplified view of travel time/distance.
  • Integration: Enhanced SAP S/4HANA Service integration, New Fiori App for viewing SAP FSM activities, Production Order integration, and item-based accounting for an unplanned service.
  • AI-based scheduling improvements: Simulate the effect of adjustments in auto-scheduling policies, and recalculate travel time for bookings.

Note: The Activity Summary, Equipment Insights, and Natural Language Filtering features require a customer to be licensed for SAP AI Units. For more information about licensing, please contact your SAP Account Executive. 

Generative AI

SAP Field Service Management Generative AI services are now available in French and German, enabling more users to access Gen AI features in their native language. In addition to simplifying workflows, generative AI in SAP Field Service Management makes it easier to unlock and benefit from valuable insights that were previously difficult to access. These enhanced workflows and actionable insights provide substantial value to field service organizations. According to SAP customer benchmarking, by utilizing these features, field service organizations can achieve (on average):

  • ⬆️ 12.5% increase in dispatcher productivity.
  • ⬆️ 5% improvement in first-time fix rates.
  • ⬇️ 15% reduction in time to review and plan service requests.

Take a look at the demo video below showcasing our AI highlights in 2411:


AI Assisted Services - Now Available in German and French

AI Assisted Services (German 🇩🇪)

AI Services in German - Intelligent Filtering and Activity Summary

 
AI Assisted Services (French 🇫🇷) 

AI Services in French - Intelligent Filtering and Activity Summary
➡️ For more information on AI services such as Intelligent Filtering, Equipment Insights, and the Activity Summary, please refer to the 2408 Release Blog. These translations are made possible using SAP Translation Hub.

AI-based Scheduling Improvements:

Simulate the Effect of Adjustments in Custom Auto-scheduling Policies

The new Policy Simulation feature allows users to test scheduling policy changes in a controlled environment, ensuring no impact on live schedules. This enhancement allows users to configure and evaluate adjustments, such as prioritizing skills levels or enforcing objectives, in a secure environment. By simulating outcomes, organizations can safely refine strategies, and ensure alignment with evolving operating conditions. Learn more about this innovation here.

Recalculate Travel Time for Bookings

Thanks to feedback from our Customer Influence Program, accurate travel times are now easier to achieve via automatic travel time recalculation. Travel times are updated automatically when planning or replanning activities, ensuring dispatchers always have precise data. This enhancement helps reduce manual adjustments, minimizes dispatcher confusion, and improves overall scheduling accuracy. Learn more about this feature here.


User Experience & Usability Enhancements

SAP Field Service Management 2411 also features several key user experience and usability highlights, which are summarized below and in the demo video:


Morning Horizon Theme

The new Morning Horizon theme in SAP Field Service Management introduces a fresh, modern design, providing users with a consistent and visually appealing interface. This standard theme enhances the user experience with improved aesthetics, accessibility, and a cohesive look and feel across all SAP applications. Learn more here.

 

SAP Feedback Survey

This provides an easy way to contribute feedback on product functionality, helping SAP Field Service Management refine and enhance its offerings based on real-world usage and customer needs. Learn more about participating here.


Dispatching Board

Configurable Header Information in the Modal UI (preview)

The new header configuration in SAP Field Service Management allows organizations to customize the key details displayed in the Modal UI for Service Calls and Activities via screen configurations. This enhancement makes it easier to display critical information by tailoring screens to meet your company’s unique requirements. Learn more about this feature here.



Manage Approvals for Worktime in the Time and Material Journal

The enhanced work time management capabilities in SAP Field Service Management improve tracking and approval of time entries through the Time and Material (T&M) Journal. This update harmonizes processes in the T&M Journal and improves workflows for work time approvals. Learn more about this feature here.

 
Option to Display the Activity Cancellation Reason 

Users can now benefit from increased transparency for cancelled activities in SAP Field Service Management, with clear visibility into the reasons for activity cancellations. For more information, visit the product documentation.


Service Call Extension Tab in the Modal UI

Extensions in SAP Field Service Management now provide greater flexibility for placement in desired outlets. The new Service Call extension tab in the Modal UI allows users to position extensions in more key locations, ensuring they are easily accessible and aligned with operational priorities. For more details, click here.

Sample Extension

 
Option to Save Column Layouts in all List Views (Preview)

The 2411 release provides enhanced flexibility for customizing column settings and layouts of the activity list. Users can easily configure visible fields and group data to align with specific operational needs and tactical scheduling scenarios, creating a more intuitive user experience with faster access to relevant data. For further info, see here.

 
Enhancements for List Views

This release introduces improved filtering capabilities, offering enhanced options for managing list views. Users can now easily filter by key information when working with a list view in the planning list, making this workflow faster and more efficient. More information, here.


Service Map

Quick Access to Activity Details

Dispatchers can now view essential activity information from the Service Map by simply hovering over an activity, reducing the number of clicks. This popup allows dispatchers to instantly view key details, and can be easily configured to display the companies specific requirements. For more details, please see here.


Service Map – Close/Dock Time-Distance Popup Box

When using the Service Map, dispatchers now can easily view detailed travel information directly on the Service Map with a simple pop-up box. This pop-up displays both travel time and distance, and can be opened, moved, and closed as needed, allowing dispatchers to quickly access and manage information without cluttering the screen. More details here.


Integration Improvements

New Fiori App for Viewing SAP Field Service Management Activities

The new SAP S/4HANA Fiori App for viewing SAP FSM activities provides a simplified and intuitive way to access activity details from SAP Field Service Management. With an organized data presentation, users can quickly navigate through activities and view critical information such as status, technician assignments, and partner data, directly from SAP S/4HANA. Get the full details here.


Cancellation of Activity from SAP S/4HANA

In SAP S/4HANA, SAP FSM activities can now be cancelled with detailed reasons, while seamlessly integrated into SAP Field Service Management. This ensures consistent data and improved transparency across both systems. For more details, refer to the help documentation.


Item-based Accounting for Unplanned Service

Item-based accounting and revenue recognition in SAP S/4HANA Service now integrates with SAP Field Service Management to support unplanned services. This enhancement ensures that revenue is accurately tracked and recognized based on actual service execution, bridging financial, profitability, and operational data for improved transparency and compliance. See the support documentation here.


Integration of Production Orders as Service Calls

Production Order integration with SAP Field Service Management enhances the connection between SAP Field Service Management and SAP S/4HANA or SAP ECC. This integration enables scheduling, execution, and confirmation of production order operations, with support for time effort, materials management, and more. More information here.

Final Thoughts

SAP Field Service Management 2411 enhances the field service value chain with multilingual generative AI, streamlined workflows, and improved integration capabilities. We invite you to explore these new capabilities, share your feedback, and help us shape the future of SAP Field Service Management by submitting your influence requests here. Stay tuned for the 2502 release, where even more innovations are on the way!

Get in touch!

Thoughts or questions? Feel free to share any feedback in a comment. It would also be great to hear directly from you which AI innovations you think could be the most impactful for future releases. For related posts and more information, please join our SAP Field Service Management Community page and explore our SAP Field Service Management Website. You can also watch the SAP Field Service Management 2411 Release Demo here, and I encourage you to register for our upcoming webinars for the most up to date information by registering here.

entry.title: GRC FFID request for multiple users is not visible for approval in Fiori
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Good day.

I have a question regarding the FFID request.

  1. When an FFID request is made for a single user, it appears in the Fiori FFID Approver tile (My Access Approvals).
  2. However, when the request is for multiple users, it does not show up in the Fiori tile, and approvers are unable to approve it. The workaround is to approve the request directly in the backend (GRC side), but approvers do not have access to do this through the Fiori tile.

    Has anyone else experienced the same issue?

    Thank you.

entry.title: Re: How to check change logs for SSCUI(Configuration Acti...
entry.content:
All changes done during the evaluation or implementation project, including changes in activities, milestones, and deployment target are tracked in the Change Logs view. Users with the role SAP_CBC_CONSUMPTION_AUDITOR have access to the Change Logs view.

Also to check the change logs for an SSC UI (Configuration activity) it's not possible through the change logs tab in CBC.

To find change logs for an SSC UI check out the following KBA - 3295974 - How to check the change logs in CBC - SAP for Me

entry.title: Unable to connect to service WebiPlugin.WebiSession from server AP01 via CMS osca:iiop:
entry.content:
We often encounter Webi report failures with the following errors:

  • "Unable to connect to service WebiPlugin.WebiSession from server AP01 via CMS osca:iiop:... "
  • "An internal error occurred while calling the 'processDPCommandsEx' API. (Error: ERR_WIS_30270)"

Occasionally, the reports will succeed, so this issue appears to be intermittent.

Not sure if this is bug or network issue. But in the event log, there is time drift warning, could this be the root cause?

Warning- The time service detected a time difference of greater than 5000 milliseconds for 900 seconds. The time difference might be caused by synchronization with low-accuracy time sources or by suboptimal network conditions. The time service is no longer synchronized and cannot provide the time to other clients or update the system clock. When a valid time stamp is received from a time service provider, the time service will correct itself.

Kindly advise.

No error found on the DB side. App and DB server OS - windows server, DB - MSSQL

entry.title: SAP ALM Roadshow LAC 2024 Wrap-up
entry.content:
We're thrilled to announce the resounding success of the SAP ALM Roadshow 2024 that recently took place in São Paulo, Brazil, and Buenos Aires, Argentina. These events were a fantastic opportunity for enterprises to dive into the latest in Application Lifecycle Management (ALM) with SAP's experts, fellow industry leaders, and collaborative representatives from various SAP Lines of Business (LoBs).

Below are some topics that we covered:

  • SAP Cloud ALM for RISE with SAP and Clean Core support strategies
  • Implementing Intelligent Enterprise with SAP Cloud ALM
  • Automating Testing with Tricentis
  • Optimizing operations combining AI and SAP Cloud ALM
  • Leveraging SAP Focused Run for Advanced Monitoring Capabilities
  • Transition from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM
  • The latest information about Change Management in SAP Cloud ALM

Key Highlights:

  1. Innovative Workshops: Attendees participated in dynamic and interactive workshops that showcased the latest SAP ALM tools and methodologies tailored for the Latin American market.
  2. Expert Insights: SAP professionals shared invaluable insights into streamlining software development processes, enhancing collaboration, and gaining knowledge on latest SAP Cloud ALM updates.
  3. Regional Case Studies: Participants heard from local success stories where companies effectively implemented SAP ALM solutions to drive business growth and efficiency.
  4. Tech Demos: Live demonstrations of advanced SAP Cloud ALM features and related solutions that can propel your organization forward.
  5. Networking Sessions: Valuable networking opportunities allowed attendees to connect with peers, share experiences, and build professional relationships.
  6. Cross-Functional Collaboration: Representatives from other SAP LoBs, such as Cloud ERP, RISE with SAP, and BTP, contributed to the discussions, providing a holistic view of SAP’s ecosystem and how it integrates with SAP Cloud ALM.

Event Participation:

  • São Paulo, Brazil: November 11, 2024, witnessed over 30 different participants, from more than 10 different customers with discussions and collaborations enriching the experience. 
  • Buenos Aires, Argentina: November 19, 2024, also saw around 29 enthusiastic participants, who engaged deeply with the sessions and content provided.

Looking Ahead: The feedback we’ve received from São Paulo and Buenos Aires has been overwhelmingly positive, reinforcing the importance of regional engagements to address specific market needs. We are excited about the ongoing conversation and collaboration within the Latin American tech ecosystem.

Conclusion: The SAP ALM Roadshow 2024 has set a new standard for innovation and collaboration in Latin America. We thank all the participants from both events, as well as our collaborative partners from Cloud ERP, RISE with SAP, and BTP, for helping make this a success. We look forward to building on this success together!!

 

 SAP Cloud ALM

entry.title: “ABAP AI is our Shared Treasure” - ABAP AI is on its way.
entry.content:
It’s time to talk about the announced ABAP AI capabilities, planned to be delivered with the 2502 release and beyond. ABAP AI is important to all SAP business software developers. It is our shared treasure that we hold in our hands. Let’s take a moment to elaborate a little while on this statement.  

ABAP AI is powerful

The new Joule copilot integrated into ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse, offers a full-fledged copilot experience and by that revolutionizes the way ABAP developers work. It provides AI support during the development process, significantly accelerating the creation and testing of custom ABAP applications. These groundbreaking developments have already been revealed and demonstrated at SAP TechEd 2024.

Imagine someone sitting next to you and guiding you, to write very good ABAP code. Not too bad, you might say. Now imagine that person complementing you and even starting to write code or to do unit tests for you. You can even discuss with this person and have them explain code you don’t understand.

Let's take a closer look at some of Joule's ABAP developer capabilities.

Joule for ABAP Code Prediction (Ghost Texting)

This feature helps you code. As you start typing your code, Ghost Text uses AI to propose the next lines of code. The difference between classic code completion already available in der development tools and this new feature is that ABAP AI actually understands the context and makes intelligent suggestions that are aligned with your coding objective. This is made possible by GenAI and SAPs trained ABAP model, which is designed to understand and predict ABAP coding patterns. This allows you to spend even more time solving complex problems and innovating.

ABAP AI Unit Agent

The ABAP AI Unit Agent is designed to assist you generate unit tests for an ABAP class. This ensures that your code is thoroughly tested. Traditionally, you would have to write unit tests manually, which is time-consuming and likely to be error-prone. With the ABAP AI Unit Agent, you can automate this process: You run the unit test for the ABAP class and check the code coverage option. The ABAP Unit Test analyzes the code coverage and identifies the methods that are not adequately covered. The ABAP AI Unit Agent adds to this the generation of unit tests.

ABAP and Core Data Services Code Explain

This feature makes ABAP, including ABAP Core Data Services (CDS), easier for developers to understand. When you select an ABAP class, CDS view, function module, a method or a code snippet, ABAP & CDS Explain analyzes the code and generates a human-readable explanation. This explanation includes a high-level overview of what the code does, as well as detailed explanations of each component. It is designed to understand ABAP coding patterns and conventions, so that it can provide accurate and useful explanations. By providing clear explanations of complex function modules, it helps developers improve their understanding of ABAP coding patterns, and best practices.

ABAP AI is attractive
SAPs ABAP AI SDK, powered by Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM ), serves as an "AI toolbox" for ABAP developers. It allows  you to incorporate AI capabilities to your custom ABAP applications. The ABAP AI SDK integrates seamlessly with the SAP BTP GenAI Hub, enabling you to leverage Large Language Models (LLMs) and infuse AI capabilities into your custom applications. This SDK is also used internally for all ABAP-based AI solutions.

With the ABAP AI SDK, you can easily access and use the models and features provided by the Hub by calling released ABAP classes and interfaces directly in your application code. The ABAP AI SDK takes care of the rest, executing prompts and handling the complexities of integrating AI capabilities into ABAP. This seamless integration significantly simplifies the process of incorporating AI into business applications. If that is not attractive, what is?

ABAP AI is a treasure

Even more powerful than the ABAP AI developer use cases mentioned above is the ABAP custom code migration use case. Our shared goal is to help our customers and partners to transform their custom code and move into SAP’s cloud universe.

ABAP AI for Custom Code Migration will be a game changer in achieving this goal.

We will help you transform your custom code by accelerating the transformation of custom code. Whether it's the mandatory transformation of ABAP code when migrating from ECC to SAP S/4HANA or the subsequent transformation of ABAP code to ABAP Cloud and a Clean Core.

ABAP AI support is provided for the migration of from the classic business suite to SAP S/4HANA and to ABAP Cloud and a Clean Core. This will include explanations of the mandatory simplification items to be considered during your SAP S/4HANA transformation, as well as code suggestions for refactoring your classic ABAP code towards ABAP Cloud. The corresponding AI features will be integrated in the Custom Code Migration App as well as in ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse.

Let us summarize: ABAP AI is powerful. ABAP AI is attractive. ABAP AI is a treasure.

 

Have a look at the ABAP AI roadmap for more information:
https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cross-product/roadmap-info/genai

entry.title: Re: SAP EWM WAREHOUSE OBD KITS WITH MATERIAL VALUE
entry.content:
Hi,

"I can extract every material picked KIT OBD's through the warehouse monitor Outbound delivery but wasn't able to find any report showing both (work order number and material value for picked material ) not sure if there's one including both "

- it is not a "work order", but a value added service order, be careful with mixing words here. There are no work efforts / values connected to any step.

- outside physical inventory EWM does not know anything about material values, so there is nothing really. I would think that it is easier to get this from ERP.

Brgds

Juergen

---
All the above is no official SAP statement.

Want to learn EWM?
Check for EWM courses: https://training.sap.com/trainingpath/Applications-Extended+Warehouse+Management-EWM+in+S4HANA
Get a SAP Learning Hub Subscription: https://training.sap.com/learninghub

And it is EWM. NOT eWM - Duh!
And if your question includes the word "transfer order" - do NOT tag the question with Extended Warehouse Management!

entry.title: Cash discount in debit memo accounting document
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have a debit memo created with reference to an invoice (f2). The product is eligible for a cash discount and hence in the debit memo condition type DCD2 is triggered (we use S/4HANA Public cloud and hence DCD2 instead of SKTO).

I noticed that in the accounting document generated for the debit memo, the line item details doesn't show the calculated cash discount amount. I can see cash discount base amount, payment terms, baseline date etc. But the discount amount itself is missing. 

If I check the accounting document of a debit memo without reference, the discount amount is available at line level along with all other details  

Is this standard behavior? Or am I missing anything here?

Any help is appreciated. 

Thank you!

entry.title: Integration architecture guide: Updated version available now
entry.content:
I am happy to share the news that an updated version of the integration architecture guide has been released! Based on SAP Business Technology Platform offerings available today, this document provides you with detailed guidance on how to evolve your existing integration architecture. As before the integration guide is structured along the concepts of the SAP Integration Solution Advisory Methodology, such as integration styles and integration use case patterns.

This blog post gives you an overview about the most important news delivered with this version of the guide.

Process Integration

This chapter has been updated to include recommendations regarding the latest capabilities of SAP Integration Suite such as the new Edge Integration Cell option, Data Space Integration and Advanced Event Mesh.

You can also find consolidated information about how to elevate toward SAP Integration Suite – be it from SAP Process Integration/Orchestration or from SAP API Management and/or SAP Cloud Integration on SAP BTP Neo cloud platform. In the first case, Edge Integration Cell plays an important role: Deployed in customer-controlled environments, it allows you to run your integration scenarios locally within your own environments, without going through the public internet. Migrated existing integrations flows can be redeployed to and run by the Edge Integration Cell.

 

Scenarios using Edge Integration Cell

Event-Driven Integration

Event-driven architecture (EDA) is a well-known approach for designing and building decoupled, hyper-connected software systems and it’s rap­idly gaining popularity in the world of enterprise integration due to its reliability, scalability, and cost-effectiveness. This chapter describes the role and interplay of SAP EDA solutions such as SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh, SAP Cloud Application Event Hub, further capabilities of SAP Integration Suite and technologies for event-enabling SAP applications.

Event-Driven Architecture in the Enterprise

Data Integration

The guide also describes the role of SAP Datasphere with regards to data integration options. SAP Datasphere enables a business data fabric architecture and supports integration use case pattern like data ingestion, virtual data access, and data transformation and orchestration.

Overview of SAP Datasphere

User Integration

There is also a major overhaul of this chapter to provide recommendations regarding the latest SAP solutions which enable a central entry point for users to business solutions. These are SAP Start, SAP Mobile Start, SAP Build Work Zone (standard and advanced edition) and SAP Task Center.

Cross-solutions entry point on SAP Business Technology Platform

Furthermore, the technology recommendation for the chatbot integration use case pattern of the SAP Integration Solution Advisory Methodology reflects now the latest innovations by introducing Joule: Joule is a generative AI-based assistant embedded in SAP’s cloud enterprise portfolio that provides proactive, contextualized insights from various data sources.

New Content Format and Access Point

In addition to updating the content of the integration architecture guide we also changed its format: The guide is now available as documentation at SAP Help Portal and comes with benefits such as easier navigation and search, option to bookmark, subscribe and to provide feedback to the content.

The integration architecture guide is an integral part of SAP BTP Guidance Framework which is the central access point for architects, developers and administrators to build and run enterprise-grade solutions on SAP BTP.

Integration architecture guide at SAP BTP Guidance Framework

The integration guide belongs to the decision guides of the framework and is interconnected with other assets of SAP BTP Guidance Framework such as the documentation of the SAP Integration Solution Advisory Methodology and SAP BTP reference architectures (for integration).

Enjoy reading the new guide! It would also be great to get your feedback, for instance which integration topics should be addressed with the next update. If you have any suggestions feel free to leave them as comments to this blog post. Happy integrating 😊

entry.title: Re: Adding Variables to Predictive Analysis
entry.content:
Hi,

I assume that by " I can’t create more than one forecast", you mean that it's not possible to get more than one future forecast point (named "forecast periods" in the user interface).

The number of forecast period is limited by the number of available past points. If you want to increase the number of forecast periods, you need to get more past data points.

This has to do with the prediction interval (the blue shaded area around the forecast that informs you about the prediction uncertainty). Forecast in chart provides a prediction only if it's able to provide a prediction interval along with the prediction. The future in the future the prediction, the more past data points are need to calculate the prediction interval. 

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Regarding to SAP ID : 103124(Clean Up Reporting Tasks)
entry.content:
In some cases, you may want to delete reporting tasks such as when inconsistent tasks get generated, or when you want to change the reporting entity settings for reporting tasks that already have runs generated. For example, when assigning periodicity of report category, you may have set the wrong values for Offset or Fiscal Year fields. This may cause inconsistent reporting tasks and runs if generated.

This would not affect the performance or the correctness of the report . This could lead to following demerits,

  • Multiple reporting tasks between the same period. For example: You have initially set the fiscal year variant for month wise (For the periodicity, 2022 to 2024) and later changed the fiscal year variant to year or quarter wise (For the periodicity, 2023 to 2024) having this variation leads to generation of inconsistent reporting tasks in run statutory reports app. In this example if clean up is not done for the overlapping period, monthly reporting tasks are generated for the period from 2022 to 2024 and quarterly or yearly reports are generated from 2023 to 2024.

 

  • This behavior is not going to affect report generation process but could lead to confusion for the user to choose the correct report tasks applicable.

 

Please refer to the KBA 3542579 - Cleanup of Reporting Tasks for Statutory reporting if you need assistance to cleanup reporting tasks.

 

Thanks and regards,

Atanu Malgope.

entry.title: Re: Would Like to Configure Rounding Values for Sales Order?
entry.content:
Hello,

Steps to Configure Rounding Values in Sales Orders:

1. **Access Configuration App:**
- Use the "Manage Your Solution" app via SAP Fiori launchpad to navigate configuration settings.

2. **Go to Pricing**
- Identify the pricing procedure used for your sales orders.



3. **Set Condition Types for Pricing in Sales*
- Look for condition types related to rounding.

4. **Define Rounding Rules:**
- Select your condition type, go to Details

- Go to Rounding Rule that specifies how rounding should behave (e.g., rounding up, rounding down, or standard rounding).

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Why SAP Cloud Identity Matters for SAP Task Center: It’s About Trust and Global User ID
entry.content:
Click, read, type, send and repeat. Approvals look quick and easy, says the promotional video of SAP Task Center.

No doubt about it. The unified inbox for approvals across multiple applications offers a straightforward and intuitive interface. The real challenge, however, lies in the initial setup of all related services and applications. Let’s see!

For employee A in your company to approve tasks from business applications X, Y, Z in the SAP Task Center, you, as an integration expert, need to configure the following components to work together:

  • SAP BTP subaccount for SAP Task Center application
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services: Identity Authentication, Identity Provisioning and Identity Directory
  • Business applications, known as task providers
  • SAP cloud connector, if task providers are on-premise systems
  • Corporate identity provider (IdP) if present in the customer landscape

This blog post focuses on the key role of SAP Cloud Identity Services in ensuring that the right users will get access to the right tasks in the SAP Task Center. It aims to clarify some misunderstandings that customers face when setting up Identity Authentication as a proxy for delegating the authentication to the corporate IdP Microsoft Entra ID and using Identity Provisioning for replicating users and groups to SAP S/4HANA acting as a task provider.

 

1.  Establish Trust with Identity Authentication

The identity and access management of SAP Task Center fully relies on SAP Cloud Identity Services (SCI). Therefore, you must configure Identity Authentication as the trusted identity provider for the SAP BTP subaccount for SAP Task Center. See Establish Trust and Federation Between SAP Authorization and Trust Management Service and Identity Authentication.

Proposed approach:

  • Use Identity Authentication as single identity provider for SAP BTP.

SAP BTP comes preconfigured with a default, SAP-managed identity provider (SAP ID service), which is different from Identity Authentication - a service that you, as a customer, can fully manage yourself. It is important to move to Identity Authentication and use it as your custom IdP. See Onboard to SAP Cloud Identity Services.

  • If more than one SCI tenants are available for establishing the trust, select the tenant that is used by your task provider (that is, SAP S/4HANA). Although customers may have several tenants for various reasons, the rule is: Your SAP BTP subaccount and all task providers must trust the same SCI tenant. Having one central tenant is key for SSO across applications.
  • Opt for the automatic, one-click Establish Trust option using the OpenID Connect protocol instead of manually exchanging SAML metadata. As a result, an OIDC application representing the SAP BTP subaccount for SAP Task Center will be created automatically in the SCI admin console.

 

2.  Things to Consider when Configuring Global User ID

The correlation between users and their tasks in the SAP Task Center fully relies on the Global User ID attribute. Therefore, you must ensure that the logged-in user in the SAP Task Center application has the same Global User ID in SAP Cloud Identity Services and all task providers.

Proposed approach:

  • Although you can provide your own unique identifier, we recommend that you generate and distribute it using SAP Cloud Identity Services. See Global User ID in Integration Scenarios.
  • The Global User ID must be included in the token issued by the IdP. SAP Task Center uses the Global User ID from the user_uuid claim in the token to correlate the logged-in users with their tasks. It does not rely on the subject name identifier (SNI) for this purpose.

Claims in the token (or assertion attributes in SAML) are defined in the application representing the SAP BTP subaccount for SAP Task Center under: Trust -> Single Sign-On -> Attributes -> Self-defined Attributes. See User Attributes.

  • In principal propagation scenarios, when the logged-in user approves tasks on the task provider side, SAP Task Center must communicate the identity of the principal (user) to the task provider, which then determines whether to permit or reject the operation.

For identifying the user, the task provider relies on the Name ID in the SAML assertion, which is populated with the value of the subject name identifier. If the subject name identifier (e.g., email) is not unique within the task provider, you can configure the userIdSource destination property in the SAP Task Center to point to the user_uuid claim in the token. Once configured (e.g., userIdsource=user_uuid), the Name ID will be determined by the value specified in userIdSource.

 

3.  Sync the Trusted IdP with the Cloud Connector

After configuring Identity Authentication as the trusted IdP for the SAP BTP subaccount, you must synchronize it as a trusted IdP in the SAP cloud connector, which serves as the link between the BTP applications and the SAP S/4HANA on-premise system. See Configure Trusted Entities in the Cloud Connector.

  • By default, the cloud connector does not trust any entity that issues tokens for principal propagation. If you forget to synchronize your IdP with the cloud connector, your users will get the 'SSO Token Validation Error' when opening their tasks in the SAP Task Center.

 

4.  Establish Trust with MS Entra ID

Configure Microsoft Entra ID as the trusted corporate IdP in SAP Cloud Identity Services. For more information, see Configure Trust with OpenID Connect Corporate Identity Provider.

  • Do not configure the SAP BTP subaccount for SAP Task Center to directly trust the corporate IdP. Instead, establish trust between the subaccount and SAP Cloud Identity Services, which should then trust the corporate IdP.

If the subaccount for SAP Task Center trusts MS Entra ID directly, that is - bypassing the Identity Authentication proxy, users will be able to log into the SAP Task Center but will not be able to open their tasks.

  • Define the corporate identity provider to be the default IdP for the application representing the BTP subaccount. For more information, see Choose a Corporate Identity Provider as Default.

 

5.  Enable Identity Federation

In this scenario, Identity Authentication is not just a proxy passing user attributes from the corporate IdP to the application during authentication. It plays a more active role by providing attributes from its own user store - the Identity Directory, to the token.

  • Configure Identity Authentication as the leading IdP for attributes sent to the application, while allowing the corporate IdP to provide additional attributes, if needed. Enable the Use Identity Authentication user store option under Corporate Identity Provider -> Identity Federation. See Configure Identity Federation.
  • Review the attributes expected by the SAP Task Center application in the SCI admin console and decide on their source and value. See Map User Attributes from a Corporate Identity Provider for Business Users.

Ensure the Identity Directory remains the source for user_uuid and groups, as illustrated in step 2. This is necessary if the IdP needs to provide information about the user's group membership to the Task Center application. For example, you might have created a user group in the Identity Directory for SAP Task Center admin users and mapped this group to the role collection that includes the TaskCenterAdmin and TaskCenterTenantOperator roles in the SAP BTP subaccount.

Note: SAP Task Center does not provide specific roles to end users for granting permissions to the application. All logged-in users have access to their task lists. If there are no tasks assigned to a user, their task list will simply be empty. By correlating users to tasks based on the Global User ID, users can only view their own list of tasks.

SAP Task Center provides roles for admin users: TaskCenterAdmin and TaskCenterTenantOperator. See Authorization Configuration.

6.  Sync Users from MS Entra ID to Identity Directory

Users authenticating to SAP Task Center must be present in the user store of SAP Cloud Identity. Therefore, you need to import/provision your MS Entra ID users to the Identity Directory. Choose one of the following options:

  • Set up Microsoft Entra ID to automatically provision and deprovision users to SAP Cloud Identity. See Configure SAP Cloud Identity Services for automatic user provisioning.
  • Use Identity Provisioning for provisioning users from MS Entra ID to SAP Cloud Identity. See Microsoft Entra ID (Source) and Local Identity Directory (Target).

 

7.  Sync Users from SAP S/4HANA to Identity Directory

In a standard scenario, customers have one system of origin for their identities and provision them to as many target SAP applications as needed using Identity Provisioning. In this particular scenario, SAP S/4HANA is the second system of origin along with MS Entra ID.

The goal of this setup - provisioning from SAP S/4HANA to Identity Directory and back to SAP S/4HANA - is to generate a Global User ID for every SAP S/4HANA user in the directory and then sync it back.

Proposed approach:

  • Start the user provisioning from SAP AS ABAP source system to the Identity Directory target system. This is needed because SAP AS ABAP serves as the user base for SAP S/4HANA. See SAP Application Server ABAP (Source) and Local Identity Directory (Target).

When using multiple sources to provision identities to one target (Identity Directory), refer to Merging Data from Multiple Sources to control which user attributes come from each source system and avoid overwriting them in the target system.

  • The default read transformation of AS ABAP does not support the display name user attribute. Since SAP Task Center requires every user to have the Display Name, E-Mail, and Global User ID, you need to modify an attribute mapping of your choice to populate it with the display name value. For example, map the ADDRESS.FIRSTNAME to the displayName as follows:

The first name of the user will be provisioned to the Identity Directory as its display name.

  • Always test your configuration with the Validate and Simulate jobs before running an actual provisioning job. For more information, see Validate Provisioning Job (verifies the content of the user) and Simulate Provisioning Job (estimates the number of provisioned users).

 

8.  Update SAP S/4HANA Users with Global User ID

Sync back the users with their newly generated Global User IDs from the Identity Directory to SAP S/4HANA. Your target Local Identity Directory from the previous steps must now be configured as a source system for provisioning to both  SAP AS ABAP and SAP S/4HANA On-Premise targets.

  • You might have created a user group, such as 'S4HANA', in the Identity Directory to group together your SAP S/4HANA users, allowing you to filter and provision only those specific users. To ensure proper filtering, refer to the supported filtering parameters in the Identity Directory API available on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub. Incorrect filtering may result in failed provisioning jobs.

 

Every integration scenario is unique and requires careful planning, customization and testing. Hopefully, this blog post offers you some guidance through the myriad of configuration options, choices, and alternatives.

entry.title: Re: Dimension data access control & Admin authorization -...
entry.content:
@Deb_D 

Roles take precedence over any Data Access Control, so if a User has a Role which has “Full Data Access” enabled, such as “BI Admin” or “Admin”, then this will take priority over any Data Access Control set on any Dimension or Public Dimension used within a Model. So yes its your Admin Rights which is superseding your DA setup. Try removing your Admin rights, or test with another user with lesser privilege roles.

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.title: Bank statement Automation CAMT.053 and CAMT.052
entry.content:
As per ISO20022 standard, by end of 2025 text format will be replaced with XML formats. Bank statement format MT940 will be transitioned to XML format CAMT.053. Usage of Payment Order option for scenarios such as

  • Non-Functional currency Payment from Functional currency bank account. How exchange rate fluctuation resulting in exchange gain/loss can be avoided
  • Supply Chain Financing: - Vendor payments are pledged with bank with agreed days
  • Customer Direct Debit : - Delay in credit on bank statement

 

User Story

Priyanaka is responsible for process payments for both domestic and cross border payments. However, the payment to be processed is a functional currency bank account. For example, when payment for non-functional currency payment is processed on 22-Nov-2024 with a value date as 25-Nov-2024. Since, the exchange rate on 22-Nov-2024 and 25-Nov-2024 are different when bank statement is received on 26-Nov-24, system was not able to clear the open items. Due to the exchange rate difference Credit to clearing bank account and debit to clearing bank account are different value in local currency.

She had also express difficulty on Customer direct debit. As customer direct debit will have a lead time in crediting on bank statement. Whereas, customer outstanding is reported incorrectly / cash flow report is incorrect. As we have credited customer and Cash flow forecast is impacted as money is yet to be received.

 

Business Requirement

  1. When a payment is processed for non-functional currency payment on 22-Nov-2024 and debited on 26-Nov-2024, instead of exchange rate gain/loss system should be able to clear open item automatically.
  2. As there is a change in ISO20022 standard in Nov-2025 text formats will be replaced with XML formats. Transition from MT940 to XML bank statement is required.
  3. Another challenge, bank is unable to provide payment details on End of the day bank statements. However, bank can give item wise payment details in another statement file.
  4. Customer direct debit, cash flow to be reported as forecasted until money is credited on bank statement

SAP Solution

ISO20022 standard transition from MT940 to XML. New XML bank statements format are CAMT.053.001.02 EOD summary and CAMT.052.001.02 is detailed line-item statements. SAP ECC version or S4 HANA version is compatible to meet the transition into XML bank statements.

Payment order Solution: -

Payment Order means that the payment program does not post a payment document and the paid items are not cleared. Instead, the system generates and saves a payment order. The information contained in this order can be used to clear the items later. The payment is posted when it appears on the bank statement, and the relevant open items are selected when the payment order is entered. The paid items remain blocked for other clearing transactions and further payment runs until the payment has been posted.

End of the day bank statement CAMT.053.001.02 will have a summary of payment orders debited on 26-Nov-2024 with exchange rate applicable on 26-Nov-24. Itemized bank statement CAMT.053.002.01 with item wise payment orders received on 26-Nov-24 with exchange rate applicable on 26-Nov-24.

When CAMT.052 is processed, customer open item will be posted without impacting the realized exchange rate gain/loss.

Customization are required are

  • Payment method to be classified as payment order
  • Define Posting rules to debit vendor and clear open item
  • External transaction assignment to Posting rule classified with interpretation algorithm 029- Payment Order Number

Payment method setup

Account symbol setup

Posting Rules setup

External Transaction determination in CAMT.053

External Transaction type -> <Domn> <Cd> + <Fmly><Cd> +<SubFmlyCd>

External Transaction type - PMNTICDTPMOR

Configuration and testing CAMT.053 and CAMT.053

https://1drv.ms/v/s!Ah2KZic1YUA_gZpe6apDUeyPmTgagQ?e=bywXSI  

Note: While processing bank statement example FF_5 t-code, enter BELNR number interval and XBLNR number interval. Payment order clear requires algorithms selection of BELNR & XBLNR number intervals. If we don't update these fields while bank statement processing, Payment order might not post.

Disclaimer

Please note this solution works if bank is sending consolidated amount on CAMT.053 and provide payments itemize in CAMT.052 statement. Family, sub family on CAMT files are only for demo purpose. Please check Family, sub family codes received on CAMT file from bank.

Conclusion

Payment order and CAMT.053, CAMT.052 solutions can be extended to following business scenarios. If bank statement formats are in text, MT940 as EOD statement and MT942 can be used

  • Supply Chain Finance: - Depending on business requirement and bank statements (XML)
  • Customer direct debit: - Customer direct debits processed are credited on bank statement (XML) on later days (T+2 days)
  • Cross border payments from local currency bank account to avoid exchange gain/loss

entry.title: SAP Business Network for Logistics 2411 Release – What’s New?
entry.content:
The 2411 release went live recently. It has been a major release with many interesting features. This blog post gives you an overview of the release highlights including:

  • a new version of the ‘Freight Orders for Confirmation’ application in SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration
  • a new way to define email rules and
  • the option to share shipment details via a public link, both in SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace.

 Please note, the blog is intended for shippers and solution owners, carriers, and data providers as well as sales and presales colleagues who already have a detailed understanding of SAP Business Network for Logistics. 

So let’s go through all those new features.

SAP Business Network for Logistics – Connectivity

Reminder: Sunset of SAP BTP Neo environment, switch to BTP Multi-Cloud environment

The integration platform of SAP Business Network for Logistics is running on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP), Neo environment. The Neo environment has been deprecated and will be sunset by December 31, 2025. A new integration platform based on SAP BTP Multi-Cloud environment is now available.

There are configuration changes required in SAP Business Network for Logistics as well as in your backend systems. The configuration apps in SAP Business Network for Logistics are now updated to accommodate these changes.

You can find our migration guides here:

  • Integration Platform Migration Guide for Shippers
  • Integration Platform Migration Guide for Carriers
  • Integration Platform Migration Guide for Solution Owners
  • Integration Platform Migration Guide for Data Contributors

Destination URLs and OAuth 2.0 token URLs for ‘Provider API for Order Events’ updated

With the integration platform migration, the destination URLs and OAuth 2.0 token URLs for Provider API for Order Events (GTT and FC were updated. When you configure the environment for trying out APIs in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, you will find the new URLs and endpoints in the following fields:

  • Starting URL: select the URL based on your data centre.
  • Resulting URL: updated upon your selection of the starting URL.
  • Token URL: updated upon your selection of the endpoint.

Endpoint: select the endpoint based on your data centre.

SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration

Contracting: ‘Freight Order for Confirmation’ – new app version for carriers

The application ‘Freight Orders for Conformation’ can be used by carriers to confirm or reject freight orders send by the shipper. A new version of this application has been launched.

When you first log onto the application after this release, you will get the following pop up.

 Later, if you also change to the other version (see picture below).

 The new version of the application comes with the following changes:

  • Freight order status have been updated (reduced from 9 to 7)
  • New filter added ‘Response Required’
  • Modified worklist tabs
  • Under the worklist ‘New’, there are now subtabs ‘All’ and ‘Updated’
  • Get a message in the worklist, when a freight order has been updated
  • View when a freight order has been updated in the ‘Freight Order Status’ column
  • Introduction of ‘Order Categorization’ (based on statuses) to identify updated orders and status 

 

 

Contracting: ‘Freight Requests for Quotations’ app – new filters

In the ‘Freight Requests for Quotations’ app, the following new fields have been added in the filter and worklist area:

  • Requested Loading Start Date Time
  • Requested Loading End Date Time
  • Acceptable Loading Start End Time
  • Acceptable Loading End Date Time

In the worklist area, the ‘Requested Loading Duration’ field has been added.

Execution: ‘Freight Orders for Reporting’ app – reporting enabled while freight order needs reconfirmation

In the ‘Freight Orders for Reporting’ app, carriers can now report events for a freight order even when they have not reconfirmed the freight order (after it has been updated by the shipper).

There is a new filter ‘Orders Pending Reconfirmation’ to select the respective orders and a message appears in the worklist to notify about updated freight orders. There is also a new tag ‘Updated’ that indicates updated freight orders in the worklist.

 

 

Please note: If reporting has already been completed and a freight order is updated by the shipper, the reporting status “Completed” remains.

 

SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace

‘Manage Models’ application: new email condition configuration tool

Solution owners can now define rules for email notifications on a configuration UI. Compared with defining rules in an event-to-action script, it’s easier and more convenient.

You can access this configuration UI by clicking ‘Email Rules’ on the model card in the Manage Models app. From there, you can:

  • create rules and define the rule settings,
  • import and export rules,
  • activate and deactivate rules.

 

 

After a rule is processed and all conditions are met, an email notification is sent to the designated recipient(s) automatically. For more, see Define the Rule on the Configuration UI in How to Send Notifications.

There is also a detailed deck on this new feature as well as a demo that you can find on the release readiness web page.

‘Manage Models’ application: Define semantic colours for custom execution status

In the ‘Manage Models’ app, you can now define semantic colours for custom execution status of shipments and freight units. The status of the following categories are shown in different colours:

  • Success (Green)
  • Inactive (Grey)
  • Error (Red)
  • Warning (Yellow)
  • Information (Blue)

This helps you distinguish between different categories of statuses and assess the situation for timely and appropriate decision making. For more, see Define My Execution Status.

‘Manage Event Logs’ application: Enhanced auto-retrigger rules

In the ‘Manage Event Logs’ app, the following new fields were added on the auto-retrigger rule configuration pop-up:

  • Postpone First Retrigger
  • Retrigger Frequency

This allows solution owners more flexibility to configure auto-retrigger rules for reprocessing messages.

For more, see Auto Retrigger Rules in Guide for Message Log Administrators.

‘Manage Message Logs’: Save personalized filter and table settings as a tile

On the worklist page, you can now save the personalized filter and table settings as a tile.

This provides easy and quick access to the data you want to see. You don’t have to apply filters and adjust table settings again. For more, see Save a Tile in the Guide for Log Administrators.

‘Track Shipments’ application: Share shipment details via public link

Solution owners can now share shipment details with business partners by generating a public link. With the link, business partners can view shipment details on a simplified ‘Shipment Detail’s page without the need for authentication and sign-in credentials. This easy access facilitates the logistical collaboration between stakeholders.

The information is only an extract of the shipment information based on the shipment details in the ‘Track Shipments’ application and is read only. For example, external code lists (e.g. for shipment status) are utilized, the tracking timeline only shows actual events and internal documents are hidden as well as mobile numbers.

Before you can share, you must enable generating public links of shipments in the event-to-action script in the ‘Manage Models’ app. Please note: the functionality is disabled per default.

For more, see Share Shipment Details via a Public Link in Guide for Transportation Planners.

There is also a detailed deck on this new feature that you can find on the release readiness web page.

‘Track Shipments’ application: Display user-defined tracking unit information

You can view user-defined information for tracking units in the:

  • Tracking units table on the ‘Shipment Details’ and ‘Freight Unit Details’ pages or
  • On the ‘Information’ tab on the ‘Tracking Unit Details’ page. (via the ‘Adapt UI’ function).

 

‘Track Shipments’ application: new filters

Solution Owners can now filter shipments by ‘Estimated Departure Date Time’ and ‘Actual Arrival Time’ and see the information in the corresponding columns of the worklist.

 

On the details page, you can also display the fields on the header or in the Information tab with the ‘Adapt UI’ function.

All Fulfillment Tracking applications: New event reason code

The event reason code TRACKING_END (Tracking End) is now supported. It indicates the tracking of the tracked process is ended and no events can be reported to it further.

 

Pease share and amplify our new features!

---------------------

The above summarizes the key innovations for 2411. We hope you enjoy the new capabilities. Thanks for reading the blog post and stay tuned! 

A full overview of the 2411 release can be found in the following link.

To learn more about SAP Business Network for Logistics visit SAP - Business Network for Logistics.

For further questions and feedback related to the blog, please check the Q&A area and feel free to post your questions in the link with the tag SAP Business Network for Logistics.

entry.title: SOLMAN_SETUPにおける、Wily Introscope未導入の影響範囲に関して
entry.content:
ご担当者様


いつもお世話になっております。


掲題の件について、今回新環境の要件としてWily Introscopeは未導入の状態でSolutionManagerを構築しております。
これに伴い、SOLMAN_SETUPにおける「管理対象システム設定 」にて、以下の設定のステータスが赤信号になっております。
これらはWily Introscope未導入による影響と判断して問題ないかをご教示願います。


　【ABAPシステム】
　　　・Introscopeホストアダプタ
　【JAVAシステム】
　　　・Introscopeホストアダプタ
　　　・バイトコードアダプタのインストール
　　　・システムレベルメトリクス生成


以上になります、よろしくお願いいたします。


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SOLMAN_SETUPにおける、Wily Introscope未導入の影響範囲に関して
entry.content:
ご担当者　様

平素よりお世話になっております。

はい、ご認識の通りです。

｢Introscopeホストアダプタ｣と｢バイトコードアダプタのインストール｣はCA Introscopeデータコレクションの一部であり、

前提条件として、Enter System Parametersステップでご選択されるIntroscope Enterprise Manager が起動している必要がございます。

アクティビティ｢システムレベルメトリクス生成｣は、システムおよびインスタンスレベルでパフォーマンスメトリクスを集約するために Introscope Enterprise Manager を構成するためのものでございます。

以上、ご不明な点等ございましたらお知らせください。

宜しくお願いいたします。

SAPサポート

entry.title: Re: Service instances created in BTP after CAP Application deployment on Cloud Foundry
entry.content:
It depends on the application which services are required. However, I'll try to give you an overview on your created services.

  • Destination Service: You need this if you want to access resources via a destination, e.g. a SAP System via destination + Cloud Connector. If your app does not access any external resources you might omit this.
  • XSUAA: You most likely need this as this enables authorization.
  • HTML5 Repository Service: This depends on your setup. There's another way to serve your HTML5 which does not require the service.
  • HDI Containers: It is odd that you have two of these. Is this intentional? The HDI container represents your database (access).

entry.title: electronic bank statement - plausibility checks for upload
entry.content:
Dear experts,

we are currently working on a ECC 6.0 and I would like to know, if it is possible in SAP standard to implement plausiblity checks for the upload of the EBS. e.g.:

- is a warning/error possible, if the same bank statement no. for the bank account is already existing in the system? Currently, the system just skips the upload for the statement without warning.

- is a error/warning possible, if the statements are not uploaded in consecuitve sequence. If statement no. 50 is missing in the system, a warning should be displayed, if the next statement no. 51 is uploaded

- is a check possible, if the opening balance of the uploaded statement doesn´t match the ending balance of the previous statement? For this check there is already the system message FB772 available. Is it enough to simply activate the message in table T100S or is there further configuration necessary?

- are there other possible checks to help with data quality?

many thanks in advance,

Hedwig

entry.title: Re: Project ID can be Automatic encoding base on project...
entry.content:
You have not mentioned your versions but by using AI4ERP, here are some general steps to review.  Please advise if you need more help:

To configure automatic encoding of Project IDs based on project type or Project Profile in SAP S/4HANA, you generally need to follow these steps in the system's configuration settings. This is a high-level overview and may require administrative access in SAP and consultation of detailed documentation or assistance from an SAP consultant:

1.Access SAP Customizing Implementation Guide (IMG): - Navigate to the SAP IMG by entering the transaction code `SPRO` in the command field. - Click on "SAP Reference IMG" to open the customization options.

2.Define Project Types: - Navigate to `Project System` > `Structures` > `Operate Work Breakdown Structure (WBS)` > `Define Project Types`. - Here you can define different project types which can be later used to categorize projects.

3.Define Number Ranges for Project IDs: - In the same area, you can specify number ranges for different project types. - Navigate to `Project System`, and under `Structures`, you select `Number Ranges for WBS Elements`. - Define number ranges specific to each project type, ensuring each has a unique range.

4.Configure Project Profiles: - Navigate back to `Project System` > `Structures` > `Operate Work Breakdown Structure (WBS)` > `Define Project Profiles`. - Each profile can be linked with a project type to ensure correct settings.

5.Assign Number Ranges to Project Profiles: - Within the same customizing path, assign these number ranges to different project profiles, linking them to the project types.

6.Set Up Automatic Number Assignment: - Under `Project System`, configure the settings for automatic number assignment. - Ensure the system settings allow for automatic assignment and that the logic links the correct number ranges with project types or profiles.

7.Assignment of Project IDs Based on Profile or Type: - Once project types, profiles, and number ranges are defined and linked, the system should automatically generate Project IDs based on the associated type or profile when a new project is created.

8.Testing: - Before rolling out, thoroughly test these configurations in a development or quality assurance environment to ensure accuracy and functionality.

9.Implementation and Training: - Implement the changes in the production environment (if applicable) and ensure users are trained on the new process. Please note: This is a broad outline and the steps might vary slightly based on your specific version of SAP S/4HANA and any customizations your organization has.

entry.title: Re: DMS inspection bath
